questions.q: Who was Kripacharya's twin sister?
questions.explanation: Kripa and Kripi were twins found together as infants and raised in Shantanu's palace, and Kripi later married Drona.

questions.q: Which king found the infant twins Kripa and Kripi and had them raised?
questions.explanation: King Shantanu of Hastinapura found the abandoned twins and reared them out of pity, which gave them their names.

questions.q: Before Drona arrived in Hastinapura, what role did Kripa hold for the Kuru princes?
questions.explanation: Kripa gave the Kuru princes their first training in arms before Drona was appointed as their master of advanced weaponry.

questions.q: Kripacharya is traditionally counted among which select group of beings?
questions.explanation: Kripa is one of the Chiranjivis, the seven immortals of Hindu tradition, along with figures like Ashwatthama, Hanuman, Vyasa, and Parashurama.

questions.q: On which side did Kripacharya fight in the Kurukshetra war?
options: He fought for both in turns
questions.explanation: Bound by his service to the Hastinapura throne, Kripa fought on the Kaurava side for all eighteen days of the war.

questions.q: Along with Kripa, which two Kaurava warriors survived the Kurukshetra war?
questions.explanation: Kripa, Ashwatthama, and Kritavarma were the only three warriors of the Kaurava army left alive when the war ended.

questions.q: After the war, which young heir of the Kuru line did Kripacharya train?
questions.explanation: Kripa lived on after the war and became the teacher of Parikshit, Abhimanyu's son and the sole heir of the Kuru line.

questions.q: From what Sanskrit meaning do the names Kripa and Kripi derive?
questions.explanation: The twins were named for kripa, meaning compassion or pity, because Shantanu took them in and raised them out of kindness.

questions.q: Whom did Kripa's sister Kripi marry?
questions.explanation: Kripi married the weapons master Drona, making Kripa and Drona brothers-in-law and fellow teachers of the Kuru princes.

questions.q: What relation was Ashwatthama to Kripacharya?
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama was the son of Drona and Kripi, which made him Kripacharya's nephew.

questions.q: Who was the father of the twins Kripa and Kripi?
questions.explanation: The warrior-sage Sharadvan, of the line of Gotama, was the father of Kripa and Kripi.

questions.q: Which apsara did Indra send to distract Kripa's father from his austerities?
questions.explanation: Indra sent the apsara Janapadi to break Sharadvan's concentration, and the sight of her led to the twins' miraculous birth.

questions.q: Where were the newborn twins Kripa and Kripi discovered?
options: On a riverbank in a basket
questions.explanation: The twins were found lying in a clump of reed grass, where they had come into being and been left in the wild.

questions.q: Why did Indra interfere with the sage Sharadvan in the first place?
options: He feared Sharadvan's growing ascetic power
questions.explanation: Indra grew alarmed at the power Sharadvan was amassing through austerity and weapons practice, so he sent an apsara to distract him.

questions.q: From whom did Kripa receive his mastery of the science of weapons?
questions.explanation: Sharadvan later returned and taught his son the complete Dhanurveda, the science of arms, making Kripa a master in his own right.

questions.q: At the tournament of the Kuru princes, what did Kripa demand of the unknown challenger Karna?
options: That he salute the elders first
That he lay down his weapons
questions.explanation: Kripa invoked the rule that princes duel only their equals and asked Karna to name his father and royal line, leaving Karna silent.

questions.q: What part did Kripa play in Ashwatthama's night raid on the Pandava camp?
options: He refused to go and stood aside
He guarded a gate and set fires
questions.explanation: While Ashwatthama slaughtered the sleepers inside, Kripa and Kritavarma held the camp gates and set fire to the camp, cutting down those who fled.

questions.q: By which patronymic is Kripa known, marking his descent from a great sage's line?
questions.explanation: Because his father Sharadvan belonged to the line of the sage Gotama, Kripa is called Gautama, and also Sharadvata after his father.

questions.q: For how many days of the eighteen-day Kurukshetra war did Kripa fight?
questions.explanation: Kripa fought through every one of the war's eighteen days and still emerged alive at its end.

questions.q: During the cattle raid on Virata's kingdom, which warrior defeated Kripa?
questions.explanation: Arjuna, emerging from his year as Brihannala, single-handedly routed the Kuru host at Virata, defeating Kripa along with Bhishma, Drona, and Karna.

questions.q: Where did Kripacharya live after the great war ended?
questions.explanation: Unlike his two fellow survivors, Kripa was received back in Hastinapura and honored in Yudhishthira's new court.

questions.q: Before the war began, what counsel did Kripa give in the Kuru court?
options: To attack before the Pandavas armed
To make peace with the Pandavas
To divide the army in two
questions.explanation: Like Bhishma, Drona, and Vidura, Kripa repeatedly urged Duryodhana to make peace and return the Pandavas their due.

questions.q: At the dying Duryodhana's request, what rite did Kripa perform beside the lake?
options: He lit the prince's funeral pyre
He swore an oath to kill the Pandavas
He consecrated Ashwatthama as the final commander
He carried Duryodhana from the water
questions.explanation: With water sprinkled in the prescribed manner, Kripa anointed Ashwatthama as the last commander of the Kaurava forces at Duryodhana's word.

questions.q: In the epic's account of divine incarnations, Kripa is said to have been born from which group of deities?
questions.explanation: The Amsavatarana section of the Adi Parva counts Kripa among those born from the host of the Rudras.

questions.q: What future role does tradition prophesy for the immortal Kripa?
options: To become one of the Saptarishis of a coming age
To be reborn as a king of Hastinapura
To guard the gates of Indra's heaven
To compose the epic of the next age
questions.explanation: Puranic tradition names Kripa among the seven great sages of the next Manvantara, alongside Ashwatthama, Parashurama, and Vyasa.

questions.q: What advice did Kripa first give Ashwatthama on the night of the planned raid?
options: To poison the camp's water supply
To rest until dawn and then fight openly
To capture Draupadi as a hostage
To burn the camp from a safe distance
questions.explanation: Kripa urged Ashwatthama to sleep, seek the elders' counsel, and attack honorably at daybreak, but Ashwatthama refused to wait.

questions.q: What unusual sign marked the birth of Kripa's father Sharadvan?
options: He was born with matted locks
He was born holding sacred grass
He was born reciting the Vedas
questions.explanation: Sharadvan was born with arrows, a sign that his destiny lay in the science of weapons rather than in Vedic study alone.

questions.q: How does the epic describe the unusual birth of the twins Kripa and Kripi?
options: From seed fallen on reeds that split in two
From a pot of ghee like the Kauravas
From a boon granted by Shiva
questions.explanation: At the sight of the apsara Janapadi, Sharadvan's seed fell on a clump of reeds and divided into two, becoming a boy and a girl.

questions.q: Immediately after the night massacre of the Pandava camp, what did Kripa and his two companions do?
options: They fled to Gandhara in secret
They set fire to Hastinapura's gates
They reported the deed to the dying Duryodhana
questions.explanation: The three survivors hurried to the lakeside where Duryodhana lay broken, told him of the slaughter, and watched him die content.

questions.q: Under whose supreme command did Kripa fight on the war's final day?
questions.explanation: On the eighteenth morning Shalya led the Kaurava remnant as commander-in-chief until Yudhishthira slew him with a spear.
